title: Sour, Crunchy Ethiopian Misir Wot
description: A traditional Ethiopian Red Lentil Stew that is both sour and crunchy from the presence of onions and a hint of vinegar, as well as the satisfying crunch of the onions and fat.

ingredients.name: vinegar
ingredients.notes: white wine vinegar or apple cider vinegar

instructions: Combine the red lentils and chicken broth in a medium saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of the lardons-render over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, for 15-20 minutes, or until the onion is softened and caramelized, stirring occasionally.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chopped heirloom tomatoes and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until they start to release their juices.
Stir in the grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cooked lentils to the skillet and stir to combine.
Stir in the vinegar and cook for 1 minute, or until the flavors have melded together.
Serve the misir wot hot, garnished with additional chopped onion and a dollop of the remaining lardons-render, if desired.
Note: This recipe can be served with a variety of sides, such as injera bread or steamed vegetables.
